mechanism_narrative: Persistently high federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and broader economic slowdown, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% (t-1mo) is near historical lows
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances following a triggered UNRATE increase, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, signaling a contractionary environment that negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 686573.0 (t-2mo) and HOUST at 1631.0 (t-2mo) are recent values
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 20-day returns in over 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST exhibit negative 3-month growth, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing sector and overall supply-side weakness, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of high inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.0107 (t-2mo) and CPIAUCSL at 303.841 (t-1mo) are recent values
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances when INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL is above 3.5% YoY, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: High and persistent inflation erodes purchasing power and increases input costs for businesses, leading to tighter monetary policy and reduced corporate earnings, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 4.0%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and healthy economic expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156204.0 (t-1mo) is a high level
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with robust retail sales indicates a healthy and expanding economy driven by strong consumer and business demand, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-year percentage change > 2.0% AND RSAFS 1-year percentage change > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20282.76 (t-6mo) and RSAFS at 686573.0 (t-2mo) are recent values
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates negative 60-day returns in over 45% of triggered instances where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong annual growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate combined with strong payroll growth indicates a tight labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and increased consumer confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 returns during periods of moderate inflation over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% (t-1mo) is low, PAYEMS at 156204.0 (t-1mo) is high
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE is low and PAYEMS growth is strong, and CPIAUCSL is below 3.0% YoY, this hypothesis is falsified.
